    Week09 leafmap

       

    Interactive Mapping in a Jupyter environment - leafmap¶

    Installation¶

    Step 1: Creating a fresh conda environment Run 'Anaconda powershell' as administrator, create a fresh conda environment using the following command:

    conda create --name name_of_your_virtual_environment

    Step 2: activate virtual environment

    conda activate name_of_your_virtual_environment

    Step 3: Install leafmap from conda-forge to the virtual environment

    conda install leafmap -c conda-forge

    Online Source¶

    Online official source

    Introduction¶

    Leafmap has six plotting backends, including folium, ipyleaflet, plotly, pydeck, kepler.gl, and heremap.

    Load basemaps¶

m = leafmap.Map(center=(40, -100), zoom=4)

    MapLibre and Leafmap - 3D Interactive Map¶

    MapLibre is a Open-source TypeScript library for publishing maps on your website.

    The MapLibre is Javascript and TypeScript based library that use WebGL to render interactive maps from vector tiles in a browser.

    Dr. Qiusheng Wu integrate MapLibre to leafmap so we can use python to generate the maps.

    Leafmap, built on top of the py-maplibregl Python package, simplifies the process of creating stunning 3D maps with MapLibre, allowing you to achieve impressive results with just a few lines of code.

    Create a API key¶

    MapTiler provide many different vector tile styles that can be used with MapLibre

    Sign up a account and generate a API Keys in your account

    • API Keys -

    Copied!

import leafmap.maplibregl as leafmap
    import os import leafmap.maplibregl as leafmap

    Time Series¶

    In [2]:

    from leafmap import leafmap
url = "https://open.gishub.org/data/landsat/timeseries.zip"
leafmap.download_file(url)

    tif_file = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), 'landsat\\1984.tif')

with rasterio.open(tif_file) as dataset:
    # Plot the image
    plt.figure(figsize=(8, 8))
    show(dataset)
    plt.title('TIFF Image')
    band_count = dataset.count
    print(band_count)
    # plt.show()
    tif_file = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), 'landsat\\1984.tif') with rasterio.open(tif_file) as dataset: # Plot the image plt.figure(figsize=(8, 8)) show(dataset) plt.title('TIFF Image') band_count = dataset.count print(band_count) # plt.show()

    Troubleshoting¶

    • No module named 'localtileserver'
    conda install -c conda-forge localtileserver
    • No module named 'rasterio'
    pip install rasterio
